seoTitle: Connect Your Domain to HubSpot: Full DNS Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to connect a custom domain or subdomain to HubSpot, match A or CNAME records, and verify SSL and propagation through the domain dashboard.
intro: Getting your own domain pointed at HubSpot means matching the right DNS records at your registrar, whether that's a root domain using A records or a subdomain using CNAME. This guide walks through HubSpot's domain wizard, generating the values you need, and updating them at your DNS provider. It closes with checking propagation so SSL activates correctly.

steps.heading: Open Domains & URLs settings
steps.body: Go to your HubSpot account settings, navigate to Content, and select the Domains & URLs directory.

steps.heading: Start the domain connection wizard
steps.body: Click Connect hosting domain to launch the setup screen for linking a new domain or subdomain.

steps.heading: Enter your domain details
steps.body: Specify your subdomain prefix and core domain, then choose which content types, like website or landing pages, it will serve.

steps.heading: Copy your DNS values
steps.body: Review the generated CNAME values or A record IPs that HubSpot needs you to point at its servers.

steps.heading: Update your DNS provider
steps.body: Log into your registrar, such as GoDaddy, Cloudflare, or Namecheap, and enter the new records while removing conflicting old ones.

steps.heading: Verify the connection
steps.body: Return to HubSpot to run a live verification check and confirm SSL provisioning completes successfully.

faq.question: Do I need an A record or a CNAME record?
faq.answer: Root domains typically use IPv4 A records, while subdomains like www or blog use CNAME records pointing to HubSpot.

faq.question: How long does domain propagation take?
faq.answer: Propagation time varies by registrar, so you may need to wait before HubSpot's verification check confirms the connection.

faq.question: Does HubSpot provide SSL automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, once your domain is verified, HubSpot handles automatic SSL certificate provisioning.

faq.question: Can I connect a domain registered outside HubSpot?
faq.answer: Yes, you just need access to update DNS records at your domain's registrar, regardless of where it was purchased.
